speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You did say so exactly. Do you now say you presume there were not sinkers at the farm?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't think there were any fish lines suitable to use at the farm; I don't think there were any sinkers on any line that had been mine.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you remember telling me you presumed there were lines, and sinkers and hooks at the farm?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I said there were lines I thought, and perhaps hooks. I did not say I thought there were sinkers on my lines. There was another box of lines over there beside mine.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You thought there were not sinkers?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Not on my lines.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Not sinkers at the farm?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't think there were any sinkers at the farm. I don't know whether there were or not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you then think there were no sinkers at the farm?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I thought there were no sinkers anywhere, or I should not have been trying to find some.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You thought there were no sinkers at the farm to be had?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I thought there were no sinkers at the farm to be had.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That is the reason you went into the second story of the barn to look for a sinker?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What made you think you would find sinkers there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I heard father say, and I knew there was lead there.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What made you think you would find sinkers there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I went to see, because there was lead there.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You thought there might be lead there made into sinkers?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I thought there might be lead with a hole in it.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you examine the lead that was down stairs near the door?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Why not?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You went straight to the upper story of the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, I went under the pear tree and got some pears first.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Then went to the second story of the barn to look for sinkers for lines you had at the farm, as you supposed, as you had seen them there five years before that time?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I went up to get some sinkers, if I could find them. I did not intend to go to the farm for lines; I was going to buy some lines here.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You then had no intention of using your own lines at Marion?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I could not get them.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You had no intention of using your own line and hooks at the farm?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What was the use of telling me a little while ago you had no sinkers on your line at the farm?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I thought I made you understand that those lines at the farm were no good to use.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you not mean for me to understand one of the reasons you were searching for sinkers was that the lines you had at the farm, as you remembered then, had no sinkers on them?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I said the lines at the farm had no sinkers.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I did not ask you what you said. Did you not mean for me to understand that?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I meant for you to understand I wanted the sinkers, and was going to have new lines.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You had not then bought your lines?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, I was going out Thursday noon.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You had not bought any apparatus for fishing?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No hooks.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Had bought nothing connected with your fishing trip?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was going to go fishing the next Monday, were you?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know that we should go fishing Monday.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Going to the place to go fishing Monday?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: This was Thursday, and you had no idea of using any fishing apparatus before the next Monday?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You had no fishing apparatus you were proposing to use the next Monday until then?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, not until I bought it.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You had not bought anything?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Had not started to buy anything?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: The first thing in preparation for your fishing trip the next Monday was to go to the loft of that barn to find some old sinkers to put on some hooks and lines that you had not then bought?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I thought if I found no sinkers I would have to buy the sinkers when I bought the lines.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You thought you would be saving something by hunting in the loft of the barn before you went to see whether you should need them or not?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I thought I would find out whether there were any sinkers before I bought the lines; and if there was, I should not have to buy any sinkers. If there were some, I should only have to buy the lines and the hooks.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You began the collection of your fishing apparatus by searching for the sinkers in the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You were searching in a box of old stuff in the loft of the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir, up stairs.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That you had never looked at before?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I had seen them.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Never examined them before?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: All the reason you supposed there was sinkers there was your father had told you there was lead in the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, lead; and one day I wanted some old nails; he said there was some in the barn.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: All the reason that gave you to think there was sinkers was your father said there was old lead in the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did he mention the place in the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think he said up stairs; I am not sure.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where did you look up stairs?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: On that work bench, like.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: In anything?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes; it was a box, sort of a box, and then some things lying right on the side that was not in the box.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How large a box was it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I could not tell you. It was probably covered up with lumber, I think.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Give me the best idea of the size of the box you can.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Well, I should say, I don't know, I have not any idea.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Give me the best idea you have.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I have given you the best idea I have.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What is the best idea you have?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: About that large, (measuring with hands.)

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That long?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How wide?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Give me the best idea you have.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Perhaps about as wide as it was long.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How high?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: It was not very high.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: About how high?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Witness measures with her hands.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: About twice the length of your forefinger?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I should think so. Not quite.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What was in the box?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Nails, and some old locks, and I don't know but there was a door knob.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Anything else?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't remember anything else.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Any lead?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, some pieces of tea lead, like.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Foil, what we call tin foil, the same as you use on tea chests?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't remember seeing any tin foil; not as thin as that.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Tea chest lead?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What did you see in shape of lead?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Flat pieces of lead, a little bigger than that; some of them were doubled together.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How many?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I could not tell you.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where else did you look beside in the box?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not look anywhere for lead except on the work bench.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How full was the box?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: It was not nearly as full as it could have been.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You looked on the bench, beside that where else?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Nowhere except on the bench.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you look for anything else beside lead?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you got through looking for lead did you come down?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir, I went to the west window over the hay, to the west window, and the curtain was slanted a little. I pulled it down.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What else?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Nothing.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That is all you did?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That is the second story of the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was the window open?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Hot?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Very hot.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How long do you think you were up there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Not more than fifteen or twenty minutes, I should not think.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Should you think what you have told me would occupy four minutes?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, because I ate some pears up there.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you think all you have told me would take you four minutes?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I ate some pears up there.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I asked you to tell me all you did.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I told you all I did.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you mean to say you stopped your work, and then, additional to that, sat still and ate some pears?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: While I was looking out of the window, yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Will you tell me all you did in the second story of the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think I told you all I did that I can remember.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Is there anything else?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I told you I took some pears up from the ground when I went up; I stopped under the pear tree and took some pears up when I went up.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you now told me everything you did up in the second story of the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I now call your attention, and ask you to say whether all you have told me- I don't suppose you stayed there any longer than necessary?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, because it was close.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I suppose that was the hottest place there was on the premises?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I should think so.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Can you give me any explanation why all you have told me would occupy more than three minutes?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, it would take me more than three minutes.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: To look in that box that you have described the size of on the bench and put down the curtain and then get out as soon as you conveniently could; would you say you were occupied in that business twenty minutes?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think so, because I did not look at the box when I first went up.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What did you do?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I ate my pears.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Stood there eating the pears, doing nothing?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I was looking out of the window.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Stood there, looking out of the window, eating the pears?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I should think so.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How many did you eat?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Three, I think.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You were feeling better than you did in the morning?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Better than I did the night before.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You were feeling better than you were in the morning?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I felt better in the morning than I did the night before.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That is not what I asked you. You were then, when you were in that hot loft, looking out of the window and eating three pears, feeling better, were you not, than you were in the morning when you could not eat any breakfast?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I never eat any breakfast.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You did not answer my question, and you will, if I have to put it all day. Were you then when you were eating those three pears in that hot loft, looking out of that closed window, feeling better than you were in the morning when you ate no breakfast?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I was feeling well enough to eat the pears.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Were you feeling better than you were in the morning?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't think I felt very sick in the morning, only- Yes, I don't know but I did feel better. As I say, I don't know whether I ate any breakfast or not, or whether I ate a cookie.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Were you then feeling better than you did in the morning?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know how to answer you, because I told you I felt better in the morning anyway.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you understand my question? My question is whether, when you were in the loft of that barn, you were feeling better than you were in the morning when you got up?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, I felt about the same.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Were you feeling better than you were when you told your mother you did not care for any dinner?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, I felt about the same.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Well enough to eat pears, but not well enough to eat anything for dinner?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: She asked me if I wanted any meat.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I ask you why you should select that place, which was the only place which would put you out of sight of the house, to eat those three pears in?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I cannot tell you any reason.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You observe that fact, do you not? You have put yourself in the only place perhaps, where it would be impossible for you to see a person going into the house?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir, I should have seen them from the front window.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: From anywhere in the yard?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, not unless from the end of the barn.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Ordinarily in the yard you could see them, and in the kitchen where you had been, you could have seen them?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't think I understand.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you were in the kitchen, you could see persons who came in at the back door?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you were in the yard, unless you were around the corner of the house, you could see them come in at the back door?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, not unless I was at the corner of the barn; the minute I turned I could not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What was there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: A little jog like, the walk turns.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I ask you again to explain to me why you took those pears from the pear tree?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not take them from the pear tree.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: From the ground, wherever you took them from. I thank you for correcting me; going into the barn, going up stairs into the hottest place in the barn, in the rear of the barn, the hottest place, and there standing and eating those pears that morning?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I beg your pardon, I was not in the rear of the barn. I was in the other end of the barn that faced the street.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where you could see anybody coming into the house?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you not tell me you could not?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Before I went into the barn, at the jog on the outside.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You now say when you were eating the pears, you could see the back door?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: So nobody could come in at that time without your seeing them?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't see how they could.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: After you got through eating your pears you began your search?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Then you did not see into the house?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, because the bench is at the other end.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Now I have asked you over and over again, and will continue the inquiry, whether anything you did at the bench would occupy more than three minutes?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, I think it would, because I pulled over quite a lot of boards in looking.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: To get at the box?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Taking all that, what is the amount of time you think you occupied in looking for that piece of lead which you did not find?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Well, I should think perhaps I was ten minutes.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Looking over those old things?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir, on the bench.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Now can you explain why you were ten minutes doing it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, only that I can't do anything in a minute.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you came down from the barn, what did you do then?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Came into the kitchen.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What did you do then?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I went into the dining room and laid down my hat.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What did you do then?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Opened the sitting room door, and went into the sitting room, or pushed it open; it was not latched.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What did you do then?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I found my father, and rushed to the foot of the stairs.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What were you going into the sitting room for?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: To go up stairs.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What for?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: To sit down.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What had become of the ironing?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: The fire had gone out.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I thought you went out because the fire was not hot enough to heat the flats.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I thought it would burn, but the fire had not caught from the few sparks.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: So you gave up the ironing and was going up stairs?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir, I thought I would wait till Maggie got dinner and heat the flats again.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you saw your father where was he?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: On the sofa.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What was his position?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Lying down.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Describe anything else you noticed at that time.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not notice anything else, I was so frightened and horrified. I ran to the foot of the stairs and called Maggie.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you notice that he had been cut?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes; that is what made me afraid.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you notice that he was dead?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not know whether he was or not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you make any search for your mother?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Why not?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I thought she was out of the house; I thought she had gone out. I called Maggie to go to Dr. Bowen's. When they came I said, "I don't know where Mrs. Borden is." I thought she had gone out.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you tell Maggie you thought your mother had come in?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That you thought you heard her come in?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you say to anybody that you thought she was killed up stairs?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: To anybody?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You made no effort to find your mother at all?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Who did you send Maggie for?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Dr. Bowen. She came back and said Dr. Bowen was not there.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What did you tell Maggie?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I told her he was hurt.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you first told her?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I says "Go for Dr. Bowen as soon as you can, I think father is hurt."

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you then know that he was dead?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You saw him?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You went into the room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Looked in at the door?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I opened the door and rushed back.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Saw his face?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, I did not see his face, because he was all covered with blood.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You saw where the face was bleeding?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you see the blood on the floor?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You saw his face covered with blood?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you see his eye ball hanging out?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: See the gashes where his face was laid open?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Nothing of that kind?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir. Witness covers her face with her hand for a minute or two; then examination is resumed.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you know of any employment that would occupy your mother for the two hours between nine and eleven in the front room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Not unless she was sewing.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: If she had been sewing you would have heard the machine?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: She did not always use the machine.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you see, or were there found, anything to indicate that she was sewing up there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know. She had given me a few weeks before some pillow cases to make.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: My question is not that. Did you see, or were there found, anything to indicate that she had done any sewing in that room that morning?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know. I was not allowed in that room; I did not see it.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was that the room where she usually sewed?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you ever know her to use that room for sewing?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Whenever she wanted to use the machine.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When she did not want to use the machine, did you know she used that room for sewing?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Not unless she went up to sew a button on, or something.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: She did not use it as a sitting room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Leaving out the sewing, do you know of anything else that would occupy her for two hours in that room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, not if she had made the bed up, and she said she had when I went down.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Assuming the bed was made?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know anything.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did she say she had done the work?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: She said she had made the bed, and was going to put on the pillow cases, about 9 o'clock.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I ask you now again, remembering that.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I told you that yesterday.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Never mind about yesterday. Tell me all the talk you had with your mother when you came down in the morning?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: She asked me how I felt. I said I felt better, but did not want any breakfast. She said what kind of meat did I want for dinner. I said I did not want any. She said she was going out, somebody was sick, and she would get the dinner, get the meat, order the meat. And I think she said something about the weather being hotter, or something; and I don't remember that she said anything else. I said to her: "Won't you change your dress before you go out?" She had on an old one. She said: "No, this is good enough." That is all I can remember.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: In this narrative you have not again said anything about her having said that she had made the bed?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I told you that she said she made the bed.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: In this time saying, you did not put that in. I want that conversation that you had with her that morning. I beg your pardon again, in this time of telling me, you did not say anything about her having received a note.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I told you that before.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Miss Borden, I want you now to tell me all the talk you had with your mother, when you came down, and all the talk she had with you. Please begin again.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: She asked me how I felt. I told her. She asked me what I wanted for dinner. I told her not anything, what kind of meat I wanted for dinner. I told her not any. She said she had been up and made the spare bed, and was going to take up some linen pillow cases for the small pillows at the foot, and then the room was done. She says: "I have had a note from somebody that is sick, and I am going out, and I will get the din- [sic] at the same time." I think she said something about the weather, I don't know. She also asked me if I would direct some paper wrappers for her, which I did.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: She said she had had a note?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You told me yesterday you never saw the note?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, I never did.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You looked for it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, but the rest have.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: She did not say where she was going?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Does she usually tell you where she is going?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: She does not generally tell me.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did she say when she was coming back?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you know that Mr. Morse was coming to dinner?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, I knew nothing about him.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was he at dinner the day before?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Wednesday noon? I don't know. I had not seen him; I don't think he was.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Were you at dinner?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I was in the house. I don't know whether I went down to dinner or not. I was not feeling well.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Whether you ate dinner or not?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't remember.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you remember who was at dinner the day before?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, I don't remember, because I don't know whether I was down myself or not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Were you at tea Wednesday night?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I went down, but I think, I don't know, whether I had any tea or not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you sit down with the family?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think I did, but I am not sure.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was Mr. Morse there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, I did not see him.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Who were there to tea?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Nobody.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: The family were there, I suppose?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir; I mean nobody but the family.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you have an apron on Thursday?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Did I what?

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have an apron on Thursday?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, I don't think I did.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you remember whether you did or not?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't remember sure, but I don't think I did.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You had aprons, of course?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I had aprons, yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Will you try and think whether you did or not?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't think I did.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Will you try and remember?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I had no occasion for an apron on that morning.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: If you can remember I wish you would.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't remember.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That is all the answer you can give me about that?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you have any occasion to use the axe or hatchet?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you know where they were?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I knew there was an old axe down cellar; that is all I knew.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you know anything about a hatchet down cellar?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where was the old axe down cellar?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: The last time I saw it it was stuck in the old chopping block.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was that the only axe or hatchet down cellar?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: It was all I knew about.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When was the last you knew of it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: When our farmer came to chop wood.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When was that?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think a year ago last winter; I think there was so much wood on hand he did not come last winter.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you know of anything that would occasion the use of an axe or hatchet?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you know of anything that would occasion the getting of blood on an axe or hatchet down cellar?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I do not say there was, but assuming an axe or hatchet was found down cellar with blood on it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you know whether there was a hatchet down there before this murder?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You are not able to say your father did not own a hatchet?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know whether he did or not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you know there was found at the foot of the stairs a hatchet and axe?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, I did not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Assume that is so, can you give me any explanation of how they came there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Assume they had blood on them, can you give any occasion for there being blood on them?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Can you tell of any killing of an animal? or any other operation that would lead to their being cast there, with blood on them?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, he killed some pigeons in the barn last May or June.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What with?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know, but I thought he wrung their necks.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What made you think so?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think he said so.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did anything else make you think so?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: All but three or four had their heads on, that is what made me think so.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did all of them come into the house?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think so.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Those that came into the house were all headless?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Two or three had them on.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Were any with their heads off?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Cut off or twisted off?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know which.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How did they look?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know, their heads were gone, that is all.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you tell anybody they looked as though they were twisted off?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't remember whether I did or not. The skin I think was very tender, I said why are these heads off? I think I remember of telling somebody that he said they twisted off.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did they look as if they were cut off?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know, I did not look at that particularly.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Is there anything else besides that that would lead, in your opinion so far as you can remember, to the finding of instruments in the cellar with blood on them?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I know of nothing else that was done.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you make any effort to notify Mrs. Borden of your father being killed?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, when I found him I rushed right to the foot of the stairs for Maggie. I supposed Mrs. Borden was out. I did not think anything about her at the time, I was so-

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: At any time did you say anything about her to anybody?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: To the effect that she was out?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I told father when he came in.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: After your father was killed?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you say you thought she was up stairs?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you ask them to look up stairs?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you suggest to anybody to search up stairs?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I said, "I don't know where Mrs. Borden is;" that is all I said.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You did not suggest that any search be made for her?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You did not make any yourself?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I want you to give me all that you did, by way of word or deed, to see whether your mother was dead or not, when you found your father was dead.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not do anything, except what I said to Mrs. Churchill. I said to her: "I don't know where Mrs. Borden is. I think she is out, but I wish you would look."

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You did ask her to look?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I said that to Mrs. Churchill.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where did you intend for her to look?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: In Mrs. Borden's room.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you went out to the barn did you leave the door shut, the screen door?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I left it shut.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you came back did you find it shut or open?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir; I found it open.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Can you tell me anything else that you did, that you have not told me, during your absence from the house?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Can you tell me when it was that you came back from the barn, what time it was?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know what time it was.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you any idea when it was that your father came home?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I am not sure, but I think it must have been after 10, because I think he told me he did not think he should go out until about 10. When he went out I did not look at the clock to see what time it was. I think he did not go out until 10, or a little after. He was not gone so very long.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Will you give me the best judgment you can as to the time your father got back? If you have not any, it is sufficient to say so.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir, I have not any.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Can you give me any judgment as to the length of time that elapsed after he came back, and before you went to the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I went right out to the barn.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How soon after he came back?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I should think not less than five minutes; I saw him taking off his shoes and lying down; it only took him two or three minutes to do it. I went right out.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When he came into the house did he not go into the dining room first?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: And there sit down?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Why don't you know?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Because I was in the kitchen.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: It might have happened, and you not have known it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You heard the bell ring?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: And you knew when he came in?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You did not see him?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When did you first see him?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I went into the sitting room, and he was there; I don't know whether he had been in the dining room before or not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What made you go into the sitting room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Because I wanted to ask him a question.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What question?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Whether there was any mail for me.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you not ask him that question in the dining room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No sir, I think not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was he not in the dining room sitting down?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't remember his being in the dining room sitting down.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: At that time was not Maggie washing the windows in the sitting room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I thought I asked him for the mail in the sitting room; I am not sure.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was not the reason he went in the dining room because she was in the sitting room washing windows?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did he not go up stairs to his own room before he sat down in the sitting room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not see him go.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: He had the key to his room down there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know whether he had it; it was kept on the shelf.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Don't you remember he took the key and went into his own room and then came back?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You don't remember anything of that kind?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir; I do not think he did go up stairs either.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You will swear he did not?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not see him.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You swear you did not see him?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You were either in the kitchen or sitting room all the time?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: He could not have gone up without he had gone through the kitchen?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you did go into the sitting room to ask him a question, if it was the sitting room, what took place then?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I asked him if he had any mail. He said "none for you." He had a letter in his hand. I supposed it was for himself. I asked him how he felt. He said "About the same." He said he should lie down[.] I asked him if he thought he should have a nap. He said he should try to. I asked him if he wanted the window left the way it was or if he felt a draught. He said "No." That is all.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you help him about lying down?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Fix his pillows or head?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir; I did not touch the sofa.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did he lie down before you left the room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did anything else take place?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Not that I remember of.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was he then under medical treatment?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: The doctor had not given him any medicine that you know of?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir; he took some medicine; it was not doctor's medicine; it was what we gave him.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What was it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: We gave him castor oil first and then Garfield tea.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When was that?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: He took the castor oil some time Wednesday. I think some time Wednesday noon, and I think the tea Wednesday night; Mrs. Borden gave it to him. She went over to see the doctor.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When did you first consult Mr. Jennings?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I can't tell you that; I think my sister sent for him; I don't know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was it you or your sister?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: My sister.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You did not send for him?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not send for him. She said did we think we ought to have him. I said do as she thought best. I don't know when he came first.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Now, tell me once more, if you please, the particulars of that trouble that you had with your mother four or five years ago.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Her father's house on Fourth street was for sale.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Whose father's house?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Mrs. Borden's father's house. She had a stepmother and a half sister, Mrs. Borden did, and this house was left to the stepmother and a half sister, if I understood it right, and the house was for sale. The stepmother, Mrs. Oliver Gray, wanted to sell it, and my father brought [sic] out the Widow Gray's share. She did not tell me, and he did not tell me, but some outsiders said that he gave it to her. Put it in her name. I said if he gave that to her, he ought to give us something. Told Mrs. Borden so. She did not care anything about the house herself. She wanted it so this half sister could have a home, because she had married a man that was not doing the best he could, and she thought her sister was having a very hard time and wanted her to have a home. And we always thought she persuaded father to buy it. At any rate he did buy it, and I am quite sure she did persuade him. I said what he did for her people he ought to do for his own children. So he gave us grandfather's house. That was all the trouble we ever had.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You have not stated any trouble yet between you and her?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I said there was feeling four or five years ago when I stopped calling her mother. I told you that yesterday.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That is all there is to it then?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You had no words with your stepmother then?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I talked with her about it and said what he did for her he ought to do for us; that is all the words we had.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That is the occasion of his giving you the house that you sold back to him?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did your mother leave any property?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Your own mother?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir; not that I ever knew of.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you ever see that thing? (Wooden club.)

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir; I think I have.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What is it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: My father used to keep something similar to this, that looked very much like it under his bed. He whittled it out himself at the farm one time.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How long since you have seen it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I have not seen it in years.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How many years?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I could not tell you. I should think 10 or 15 years; not since I was quite a little girl, if that is the one. I can't swear that it is the one; it was about that size. (Marks it with a cross.)

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How many years, 10 or 15?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I was a little girl, it must be as much as that.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When was the last time the windows were washed before that day?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Why don't you know?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Because I had nothing to do with the work down stairs.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When was the last time that you ate with the family, that you can swear to, before your mother was killed?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Well, I ate with them all day Tuesday, that is, what little we ate we sat down to the table; and I think I sat down to the table with them Wednesday night, but I am not sure.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: All day Tuesday?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I was down at the table.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I understand you to say you did not come down to breakfast?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: That was Wednesday morning.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I understood you to say that you did not come down to breakfast?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I came down, but I did not eat breakfast with them. I did not eat any breakfast. Frequently I would go into the dining room and sit down to the table with them and not eat any breakfast.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you give to the officer the same skirt you had on the day of the tragedy?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you know whether there was any blood on the skirt?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Assume that there was, do you know how it came there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you any explanation of how it might come there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you know there was any blood on the skirt you gave them?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Assume that there was, can you give any explanation of how it came there, on the dress skirt?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Assume that there was, can you suggest any reason how it came there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you offered any?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you ever offered any?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you said it came from flea bites?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: On the petticoats I said there was a flea bite. I said it might have been. You siad [sic] you meant the dress skirt.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I did. Have you offered any explanation how that came there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I told those men that were at the house that I had had fleas; that is all.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you offer that as an explanation?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I said that was the only explanation that I knew of.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Assuming that the blood came from the outside, can you give any explanation of how it came there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You cannot now?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What shoes did you have on that day?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: A pair of ties.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What color?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Black.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Will you give them to the officer?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where are they?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: At home.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What stockings did you have on that day?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Black.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where are they?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: At home.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have they been washed?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Will you give them to the officer?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: The window you was at is the window that is nearest the street in the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir; the west window.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: The pears you ate you got from under the tree in the yard?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How long were you under the pear tree?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think I was under there very nearly four or five minutes. I stood looking around. I looked up at the pigeon house that they have closed up. It was no more than five minutes, perhaps not as long. I can't say sure. (Judge Blaisdell.) Was this witness on Thursday morning in the front hall or front stairs or front chamber, any part of the front part of the house at all?

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What do you say to that?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I had to come down the front stairs to get into the kitchen.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you came down first?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Were you afterwards?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Not at all?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Except the few minutes I went up with the clean clothes, and I had to come back again.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That you now say was before Mr. Borden went away?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: (Mr. Knowlton.) Is there anything you would like to correct in your previous testimony?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you buy a dress pattern in New Bedford?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: A dress pattern?

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Yes.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think I did.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where is it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: It is at home.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Where at home?

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Please.

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: It is in a trunk.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: In your room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir; in the attic.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Not made up?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Oh, no, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where did you buy it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know the name of the store.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: On the principal street there?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think it was on the street that Hutchinson's book store is on. I am not positive.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What kind of a one was it, please?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: It was a pink stripe and a white stripe, and a blue stripe corded gingham.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Your attention has already been called to the circumstance of going into the drug store of Smith's, on the corner of Columbia and Main streets, by some officer, has it not, on the day before the tragedy?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know whether some officer has asked me, somebody has spoken of it to me; I don't know who it was.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did that take place?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I [sic] did not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you know where the drug store is?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you go into any drug store and inquire for prussic acid?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where were you on Wednesday morning that you remember?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: At home.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: All the time?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: All day, until Wednesday night.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Nobody there but your parents and yourself and the servant?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Why, Mr. Morse came sometime in the afternoon, or at noon time, I suppose, I did not see him.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: He did not come so to see you?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir, I did not see him.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: He did not come until afternoon anyway, did he?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't think he did; I am not sure.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you dine with the family that day?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I was down stairs, yes, sir. I did not eat any breakfast with them.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you go into the drug store for any purpose whatever?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I think you said yesterday that you did not go into the room where your father lay, after he was killed, on the sofa, but only looked in at the door?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I looked in; I did not go in.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You did not step into the room at all?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you ever, after your mother was found killed, go into that room?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you afterwards go into the room where your father was found killed, any more than to go through it to go up stairs?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: When they took me up stairs they took me through that room.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Otherwise than that did you go into it?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Let me refresh your memory. You came down in the night to get some water with Miss Russell, along towards night, or in the evening, to get some water with Miss Russell?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Thursday night? I don't remember it.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Don't you remember coming down sometime to get some toilet water?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir; there was no toilet water down stairs.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Or to empty the slops?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't know whether I did Thursday evening or not. I am not sure.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You think it may have been some other evening?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't remember coming down with her to do such a thing. I may have, I can't tell whether it was Thursday evening or any other evening.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Other than that, if that did take place, you don't recollect going into that room for any purpose at any time?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was the dress that was given to the officers the same dress that you wore that morning?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: The India silk?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, it is not an India silk, it is silk and linen; some call it bengaline silk.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Something like that dress there? (Pongee.)

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, it was not like that.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you give to the officer the same shoes and stockings that you wore?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I did, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you remember where you took them off?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I wore the shoes ever after that, all around the house Friday, and all day Thursday, and all day Friday and Saturday until I put on my shoes for the street.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That is to say you wore them all that day, Thursday, until you took them off for the night?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you tell us yesterday all the errand that you had at the barn?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You have nothing to add to what you said?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You had no other errand than what you have spoken of?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Miss Borden, of course you appreciate the anxiety that everybody has to find the author of this tragedy, and the questions that I put to you have been in that direction; I now ask you if you can furnish any other fact, or give any other, even suspicion, that will assist the officers in any way in this matter?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: About two weeks ago-

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Was you going to tell the occurrence about the man that called at the house?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir. It was after my sister went away. I came home from Miss Russell's one night, and as I came up, I always glanced towards the side door as I came along by the carriage way, I saw a shadow on the side steps. I did not stop walking, but I walked slower. Somebody ran down the steps, around the east end of the house. I thought it was a man, because I saw no skirts, and I was frightened, and of course I did not go around to see. I hurried in the front door as fast as I could and locked it.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What time of night was that?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think about quarter of 9; it was not after 9 o'clock, anyway.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you remember what night that was?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir; I don't. I saw somebody run around the house once before last winter.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: One thing at a time. Do you recollect about how long ago that last occurrence was?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: It was after my sister went away. She has been away two weeks today, so it must have been within two weeks.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Two weeks today? Or two weeks at the time of the murder?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Is not today Thursday?

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Yes, but I thought you said she was gone two weeks the day of the murder?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Is not today Thursday?

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Yes, but that would be three weeks. I thought you said the day your father was murdered she had been away just two weeks?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, she had.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Then it would be three weeks today your sister went away, a week has elapsed?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, it would be three weeks.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You mean it was sometime within the two weeks that your sister was away?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes. I had forgotten that a whole week had passed since the affair.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Different from that you cannot state?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir; I don't know what the date was.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: This form when you first saw it was on the steps of the backdoor?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Went down the rear steps?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Went down towards the barn.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Around the back side of the house?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Disappeared in the dark; I don't know where they went.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you ever mentioned that before?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir; I told Mr. Jennings.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: To any officer?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't think I have, unless I told Mr. Hanscomb.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What was you going to say about last winter?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Last winter when I was coming home from church one Thursday evening I saw somebody run around the house again. I told my father of that.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you tell your father of this last one?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Of course you could not identify who it was either time?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: No, I could not identify who it was, but it was not a very tall person.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you sealskin sacks?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where are they?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Hanging in a large white bag in the attic, each one separate.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Put away for the summer?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Do you ever use prussic acid on your sacks?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Acid? No, sir; I don't use anything on them.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Is there anything else that you can suggest that even amounts to anything whatever?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I know of nothing else except the man who came, and father ordered him out, that is all I know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: That you told about the other day?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think I did; yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You have not been able to find that man?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I have not; I don't know whether anybody else has or not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you caused search to be made for him?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: Yes, sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When was the offer of reward made for the detection of the criminals?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I think it was made Friday.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Who suggested that?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: We suggested it ourselves, and asked Mr. Buck if he did not think it was a good plan.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Whose suggestion was it, yours or Emma's?

speaker: Lizzie Borden
testimony: I don't remember. I think it was mine.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What is your name?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: John V. Morse.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What does the V. stand for?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Vinnicum.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What is your place of residence, Mr. Morse?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: For the last year it has been South Dartmouth; my real home is in the West.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Wherebouts in South Dartmouth?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: William A. Davis.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I shall have to ask you many questions.

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I will answer any questions.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What is your business?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: When I am home, farming.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Here?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Nothing particular. I have been helping Mr. Davis some in the meat business he is in there.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you had any connection with the horse business?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Not recently. I brought some horses here when I came two years and a half ago.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: All sold out now?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you had any dealings in horses since then?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: A little along occasionally, not to amount to anything.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What relation is Mr. Davis to you?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: None. Years before I went West, I worked for them in the meat business. I have always kept up correspondence since. It seems like home to me, and I like to stay there. Isaac C. Davis, his son, is in the meat business with him. The old man cannot see now, has a cancer. I stay there with them.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Are you a married man?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Never have been?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: This question you are not obliged to answer, unless you want to. Are you a man of some property?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Enough to live on without working?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What relation were you to Mr. Borden?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Mr. Borden's first wife was my sister.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What was her name?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Sarah A. Morse.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How long ago did she die?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Well, I cant tell exactly, but somewhere during War Times.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How old was her youngest child?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: About 30. I cant tell exactly.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: No, when she died?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I think about three years old.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How long have you been in the west?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Since I first went West?

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Yes.

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I think about 38 years.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What part of the west?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: When I first went West I went to Minnesota, stayed about a year, and came from there to Macoupin County Illinois.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where have you spent the largest portion of your time?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: The largest portion I guess in Iowa, Hastings, Mills County.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Is that your last residence in the West?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes, my property is there.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you come here with the intention of remaining here?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I rented my farm for one year. I did not know how long I might stay. I calculated to stay, then I rented it for another year.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Where did you come to here first?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Warren, Rhode Island.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Who did you know there?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I have an Uncle there.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: No relative of Mrs. Borden?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No, brother to my father.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: On the other side?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Then that is Uncle to Mrs. Borden too?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What is his name?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Charles.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Charles Borden?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No, Morse.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How long did you stay there?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I guess about a year and a half.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When did you first come to see Mr. A. J. Borden?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: After I came back this time from the West, do you mean?

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Yes.

speaker: John Morse
testimony: A few weeks after, I cant tell just when.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You were on terms of acquaintance with him and his family, I suppose?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Had you visited him during your absence in the West, or had you been East during that time?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I dont understand.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Had you been East during the 38 years?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: O, yes. I came the first time just after the War, I guess the same year the War ended. Then I was here the year before the Centennial, and the year afterwards; and I were here, I think seven years ago previous to this. I think those are all the times.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: On those visits did you come to the Bordens too?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Always stayed there; at one time nearly a year of the time.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Have you kept up a correspondence with the Bordens?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: During the whole time?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: That is, with his oldest daughter.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Which one is that?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Emma.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Never with any of the rest of them?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I used to have a letter occasionally from my brother in law.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: From Borden himself?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How frequent was the correspondence with Miss Emma?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Probably once in three or four months.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When you came back, after you had been here a short time, you came to see them, this time?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes. When I first came, my horses were troublesome, I could not come for three or four weeks.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How often did you come to see them after that? Are you a trifle hard of hearing?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No, I thought I could hear as well as any one.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How often did you come to see them after that?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Sometimes once a week; sometimes once in three or four weeks; sometimes once in three months, just as it happened.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you often stay over night?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes Sir, quite often.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Were you on good terms with all the family?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: The last time you were there before this murder was when?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I should think somewhere about the 10th of July.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: How long did you stay then?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I did not stay but a short time. I was here over night, but I went down to an Aunt's on the Stafford Road at that time.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: What is her name?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Catherine Boudray.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Before that, can you recall the last time you were there?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: It was somewhere the last of June. I know Phebe Curry was sick at that time, she died a little afterwards, I think about the last of June.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you stop all night then?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No Sir, came over in the morning and went back at night. I can tell all about that time if you want me to. There was a lady came over, Mr. Davis' daughter, with me. We drove over in the afternoon. I hired a horse, and Mr. Borden's daughter went to ride, we went down to the steam boat. I took her home after dark.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you see much of Miss Lizzie when you came to the house?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Sometimes; sometimes I did not see either of the girls, stayed a few minutes and talked with Mr. Borden, and went out.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Take the last time but one in July, when you went down to Boudray's, did you see Miss Lizzie then?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I dont think I saw either of them at that time.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Take the time before that, when Miss Davis came over with you, did you see the girls then?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I saw Emma; I went to ride, I told you.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you see Lizzie?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I dont think I did.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: The day of the tragedy, the visit on the occasion when the murder happened, did you see her?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No. I came here about half past one a week ago today, I think it was.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did you see Miss Lizzie before the murder?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No, I did not.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Had you often stopped over night?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Why, occasionally.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Had you seen much of the state of the domestic relations in the family?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No. I dont know but I saw.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: So you could speak with any positiveness as to the relations between Lizzie and her mother?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: They were always on good terms, as far as I know.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: I might make the same remark. Did you see enough of her, when you were there, and of her relations, to speak with positiveness as to what her relations were with her mother?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I should think they were pleasant. She used to eat to the table with her, and I did not see anything.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: Did she usually eat with her?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Occasionally sometimes in the morning she would not eat there, probably would not be up.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: The last three times you were there, you did not see Miss Lizzie at all?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I dont think I did.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You ate meals there?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: The last time I ate dinner there, the last time I was there, that is all.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You did not see her then?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I take that back, dinner and breakfast.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You did not see her then?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No Sir.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: The time before, did you eat there?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No, I think I went down to Aunt's.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When Miss Davis came over, did you eat there then?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes, we took dinner and supper.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: And did not see Miss Lizzie then?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: No.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: When is the last time you remember eating at the table with Miss Lizzie, before the tragedy?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: I dont know as I can call to mind.

speaker: Hosea Knowlton
testimony: You have done so?

speaker: John Morse
testimony: Yes.
